description: Current time expressed in 24‑hour clock.
Time of the indicated date variable, expressed in 24‑hour clock.
Current time expressed as a 12‑hour clock.
Time of the indicated date variable, expressed as a 12‑hour clock.
Hour of the current time, expressed in a 12‑hour clock.
Hour of the indicated date variable's time, expressed in a 12‑hour clock.
Hour of the current time, expressed in a 24‑hour clock.
Hour of the indicated date variable's time, expressed in a 24‑hour clock.
Minute of the current time.
Minute of the indicated date variable's time.
Second of the current time.
Second of the indicated date variable's time.
Millisecond of the current time.
Millisecond of the indicated date variable's time.
AM/PM designation of the current time (or proper designation in the set culture).
AM/PM designation of the indicated date variable's time.
Current date/time as a four‑digit year, two‑digit month, two‑digit day, two‑digit hour (24‑hour), two‑digit minute, two‑digit second and three‑digit millisecond (e.g., 20220101123020500).
Timestamp of the indicated date variable's date/time.
Current date formatted as 'April 3, 2022'.
Indicated date variable formatted as 'April 3, 2022'.
Current year as a number (2020, 2021, …).
Indicated date variable's year as a number.
Current day of the month as a number.
Indicated date variable's day of the month as a number.
Current month spelled out ('April', 'May', …).
Indicated date variable's month spelled out.
Current month as a number (April = 4, May = 5, …).
Indicated date variable's month as a number.
Current day of the week spelled out ('Monday', 'Tuesday', …).
Indicated date variable's day of the week spelled out.
Current date as ticks (numeric value expressed as text).
Indicated date variable as ticks.
Current date/time, formatted using standard format strings from a text variable. Example: {DATETIMEFORMAT:myFormat} with myFormat set to 'd MMMM yyyy' renders '4 May 2022'.
Indicated date/time variable, formatted using standard format strings from a text variable.
Generates a random integer between lowValue and highValue (inclusive). Example: {RANDOM:1:100} picks a number between 1 and 100.
Generates a random decimal between lowValue and highValue (inclusive). Decimal places determined by the provided values.
Evaluates an arithmetic, text, or logical expression. Supports +, -, *, /, %, comparisons, AND, OR, NOT, LIKE, TRIM, SUBSTRING, LEN, IIF. Uses invariant culture for decimals.
Same as {EXP} but renders decimal values using the invariant culture (point as decimal separator). Recommended for sharing profiles across locales.
Retrieves the value of a text variable. Returns 'Not set' if the variable is not set.
Retrieves the text variable's value; if not set, uses the provided default value.
Retrieves the text variable's value URL-encoded.
Randomly selects one of the semicolon‑separated items in the value. Example: {TXT RANDOM:food;animals;jewelry}.
Returns the length of the text variable's value. Can also process a literal value or token enclosed in double quotes.
Returns the text variable's value in upper case. Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Returns the text variable's value in lower case. Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Removes leading and trailing spaces from the text variable's value. Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Replaces all occurrences of text in variableFrom with text in variableTo within the source text. Can use literal strings in double quotes.
Replaces text matched by a regular expression (in variableFrom) with variableTo within the source text.
Removes all non‑numeric characters (except '.', '-'). Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Removes all numeric characters (0‑9). Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Replaces numeric words (e.g., 'one' → '1') with integer representations. Optional selection parameter for custom word lists (comma‑delimited, ten items starting with 'zero').
Attempts to title‑case the value (e.g., 'war and peace' → 'War And Peace'). Can process literal/token in double quotes.
Concatenates two text values (variables or literals). Returns 'Not set' if either is not set.

token: {TXTSUBSTR:textVariableOf / text value:intVariableBegin / int value : intVariableLength / int value}
description: Returns a substring from textVariableOf starting at intVariableBegin (zero‑based) with given length. Can use variables, literals, or tokens.

token: {TXTPOS:textVariableOf / text value:textVariableIn / text value : intVariableStart / int value}
description: Returns the zero‑based position of textVariableOf within textVariableIn, starting from intVariableStart. Case‑sensitive; returns -1 if not found.

token: {TXTLASTPOS:textVariableOf / text value:textVariableIn / text value : intVariableStart / int value}
description: Returns the last zero‑based position of textVariableOf within textVariableIn, searching from the end. Returns -1 if not found.
Returns the number of times textVariableOf appears in textVariableIn (case‑sensitive).
Retrieves the value of a small integer variable (formerly 'condition'). Returns 'Not set' if not set.
Retrieves the small integer variable's value; uses default if not set.
Same as {SMALL} but formatted with commas (for TTS).
Same as {SMALLFORMAT} with a default value.
Retrieves the value of an integer variable. Returns 'Not set' if not set.
Retrieves the integer variable's value; uses default if not set.
Same as {INT} but formatted with commas.
Same as {INTFORMAT} with a default value.
Retrieves the value of a decimal variable using the current culture. Returns 'Not set' if not set.
Retrieves the decimal variable's value; uses default if not set.
Retrieves the decimal variable's value using the invariant culture (point as decimal separator).
Same as {DECINV} with a default value.
Retrieves the value of a Boolean variable as 'True' or 'False'. Returns 'Not set' if not set.
Retrieves the Boolean variable's value; uses default if not set.
Retrieves the value of a date/time variable as a formatted date string. (Implied; also variants like {DATE:dateVariableName} for formatting.)
Retrieves the date variable's value; uses default if not set.
Renders as a single space character.
Renders as a newline character.
Renders as a tab character.
Renders as a single double‑quote character.
Renders a new GUID (36‑character unique identifier).
Renders a GUID without dashes (32‑character unique identifier).
Renders the ASCII character for the given value (0‑127). Returns empty if invalid.
Returns the active window's title text.
Returns the active window's process name (as seen in Task Manager).
Returns the active window's process ID.
Returns the full path of the active window's executable.
Returns the active window's width in pixels.
Returns the active window's height in pixels.
Returns the active window's top (Y coordinate).
Returns the active window's left (X coordinate).
Returns the active window's right (left + width).
Returns the active window's bottom (top + height).
Returns '1' if a process with the name specified in textVariable exists (supports wildcards).
Returns the number of processes with the given name (supports wildcards).
Returns '1' if a process with a main window matching the title is the foreground window (supports wildcards).
Returns '1' if a process with a main window matching the title is minimized.
Returns '1' if a process with a main window matching the title is maximized.
Returns '1' if a window with the given title exists (supports wildcards).
Returns '1' if a window with the given title is the foreground window.
Returns '1' if a window with the given title is minimized.
Returns '1' if a window with the given title is maximized.
Returns the number of windows with the given title (supports wildcards).
Returns the title of the window currently under the mouse.
Returns the process name of the window currently under the mouse.
Returns '1' if the current command's target window is the foreground window.
Returns '1' if the current command's target window is minimized.
Returns '1' if the current command's target window is maximized.
Returns the X coordinate of the mouse relative to the entire screen (0 = top‑left).
Returns the Y coordinate of the mouse relative to the entire screen.
Returns the X coordinate of the mouse relative to the active window (0 = top‑left).
Returns the Y coordinate of the mouse relative to the active window.
Returns '1' if Caps Lock is on, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Num Lock is on, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Scroll Lock is on, '0' otherwise.
Replaced by the current text content of the Windows clipboard.
Replaced by the entire dictation buffer content without formatting.
Replaced by the dictation buffer with formatting options: PERIOD, CAPITAL, LATEST, UPPERCASE, LOWERCASE, NEWLINE, SPACEX. Example: {DICTATION:PERIOD:CAPITAL}.
Returns '1' if dictation mode is on, '0' if off. (Duplicate token name but different usage.)
Returns '1' if the specified keyboard key is pressed, '0' otherwise. Key can be any letter, number, or special key (ENTER, F10, LCTRL, etc.).
Returns '1' if any keyboard key is currently pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns a text description of a Windows virtual key code (e.g., 65 → 'A' on U.S. keyboard).
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack's keyboard hook is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack's keyboard shortcuts are enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the left mouse button is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the right mouse button is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the middle mouse button is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the forward mouse button is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the back mouse button is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if any of the five standard mouse buttons is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack's mouse hook is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack's mouse shortcuts are enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns the number of seconds (up to two decimals) since the last Windows input.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack is listening, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack's joystick shortcuts are enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns the current audio level from the speech engine (0‑100).
Returns the full path of the last played audio file.
Returns the number of currently playing audio files. (0 if legacy audio is used.)
Returns the number of currently playing instances of a specific audio file (by path).
Returns the current position (in seconds) of a playing audio file. Returns 0 if not playing.
Returns the selected audio output type: 'Legacy', 'Windows', or 'Integrated'.
Returns the number of currently playing text‑to‑speech instances. Returns -1 on error.
Returns the name of the default multimedia playback device.
Returns the number of active playback devices. Returns -1 on error.
Returns the name of a playback device by its ordinal number (0‑based).
Returns the name of the default communications playback device.
Returns the name of the default multimedia recording device.
Returns the number of active recording devices. Returns -1 on error.
Returns the name of a recording device by its ordinal number.
Returns the name of the default communications recording device.
Returns the system volume (default playback device) as a number 0‑100.
Returns '1' if the system volume is muted, '0' otherwise.
Returns the microphone volume (default recording device) as a number 0‑100.
Returns '1' if the microphone is muted, '0' otherwise.
Returns the volume (0‑100) of an application as seen in the System Volume Mixer, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns '1' if the specified application is muted in the System Volume Mixer, '0' otherwise, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns '1' if the speech engine's recording device is muted, '0' otherwise.
Returns the volume of the speech engine's recording device (0‑100).
Returns the culture name of the current speech engine. Empty if speech is disabled.
Returns '1' if using a user‑installed Speech Platform 11 engine, '0' if built‑in.
Returns '1' if speech is currently detected by the engine, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if speech recognition is enabled and available, '0' otherwise.
Returns the system directory path (e.g., 'C:\Windows\System32').
Returns the Windows directory path (e.g., 'C:\Windows').
Returns the value of a Windows environment variable (e.g., {STATE_ENV:programfiles}).
Returns '1' if the specified directory exists, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the specified directory contains files, '0' otherwise.
Returns the default user locale (culture) of the system.
Returns the default user interface language.
Returns the average CPU usage (0‑100) across all cores.
Returns the CPU usage (0‑100) for a specific core.
Returns the total system RAM in bytes.
Returns the available system RAM in bytes.
Returns '1' if the specified file exists, '0' otherwise.
Returns the full VoiceAttack version string (e.g., '1.5.12.15').
Returns the major version component.
Returns the minor version component.
Returns the build version component.
Returns the revision version component.
Returns '1' if the version is a release (not beta), '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the current VoiceAttack version is >= the version specified, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if plugin support is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if nested tokens are enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if VoiceAttack is running as a 64‑bit process, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if dark mode is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the log displays new entries at the bottom, '0' if at the top.
Returns '1' if Joystick 1 is enabled in VoiceAttack, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 2 is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 3 is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 4 is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if any joystick is enabled, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 1 is configured as a Gamepad Controller, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 2 is configured as a Gamepad Controller, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 3 is configured as a Gamepad Controller, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if Joystick 4 is configured as a Gamepad Controller, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the specified button on Joystick 1 is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the specified button on Joystick 2 is pressed.
Returns '1' if the specified button on Joystick 3 is pressed.
Returns '1' if the specified button on Joystick 4 is pressed.
Returns '1' if any button on Joystick 1 is pressed, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if any button on Joystick 2 is pressed.
Returns '1' if any button on Joystick 3 is pressed.
Returns '1' if any button on Joystick 4 is pressed.
Returns '1' if POV (hat) is enabled for Joystick 1, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if POV is enabled for Joystick 2.
Returns '1' if POV is enabled for Joystick 3.
Returns '1' if POV is enabled for Joystick 4.
Returns the POV type for a specific POV controller (X=stick, Y=POV number). Values: -1 (unavailable), 0 (off), 1 (on/off switch), 2 (up/down), 3 (left/right), 4 (four‑way), 8 (eight‑way).
Returns the direction of a POV controller (e.g., 'UP', 'RIGHT', 'DOWNLEFT') or '-1' if unavailable.
Returns the numeric POV angle (0‑35999) or -1 when centered.
Returns the X‑axis value of Joystick 1 (0‑65535), or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the Y‑axis value of Joystick 1.
Returns the Z‑axis value of Joystick 1.
Returns the rotation X value of Joystick 1.
Returns the rotation Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the rotation Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the acceleration X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the acceleration Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the acceleration Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the angular acceleration X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the angular acceleration Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the angular acceleration Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the angular velocity X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the angular velocity Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the angular velocity Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the force X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the force Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the force Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the torque X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the torque Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the torque Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the velocity X value of Joystick 1, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the velocity Y value of Joystick 1.
Returns the velocity Z value of Joystick 1.
Returns the slider value for slider Y on stick X, or -1 if unavailable.
Returns the acceleration slider value for slider Y on stick X.
Returns the force slider value for slider Y on stick X.
Returns the velocity slider value for slider Y on stick X.
For gamepad controllers, returns the left trigger position (0‑255) or -1 if unavailable.
For gamepad controllers, returns the right trigger position (0‑255) or -1 if unavailable.
Left trigger for Joystick 2 gamepad.
Right trigger for Joystick 2 gamepad.
Left trigger for Joystick 3 gamepad.
Right trigger for Joystick 3 gamepad.
Left trigger for Joystick 4 gamepad.
Right trigger for Joystick 4 gamepad.
The VoiceAttack installation directory.
The VoiceAttack Shared\Assemblies folder (no existence check).
The VoiceAttack Sounds folder (configurable in Options).
The VoiceAttack Apps folder (configurable in Options).
The name of the currently executing command (spoken phrase or hotkey name).
Returns a specific portion (zero‑based) of a dynamic command. Example: {CMDSEGMENT:1} for the second segment.

Returns how the command was executed: 'Spoken', 'Keyboard', 'Joystick', 'Mouse', 'Profile', 'External', 'Unrecognized', 'ProfileUnloadChange', 'ProfileUnloadClose', 'DictationRecognized', 'Plugin', 'Other'.
When using wildcards, returns the text before the wildcard phrase.
When using wildcards, returns the text after the wildcard phrase.
When using wildcards, returns the actual wildcard text that matched.
Returns the speech engine's confidence level (0‑100) for the recognized command.
Returns the minimum confidence level set for this command (0‑100).
Returns the last spoken command phrase.
Returns the spoken command phrase before the last one.
Returns the spoken command from history (0 = last, 1 = previous, etc.). Up to 1000 entries.
Returns the last captured speech text (recognized or unrecognized). Always lowercase.
Returns the previous captured speech text.
Returns '1' if the current command is a subcommand, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the command was invoked by a double tap, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the command was invoked by a long press, '0' otherwise.
Returns the number of top‑level commands executed since VoiceAttack started.
Returns the full 'When I Say' text of the command.
Returns '1' if the specified command is currently executing, '0' otherwise.
Returns the number of active instances of the specified command.
Returns '1' if the specified command exists in the active profile, '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the current command is already executing in another instance.
Returns the number of seconds since the last user‑initiated command (not subcommands).
In an 'Execute a command each time a command phrase is recognized' command, returns '1' if the phrase was rejected for low confidence but still executed.
Returns '1' if the command was invoked via a listening override keyword (e.g., 'Computer, …'), '0' otherwise.
Returns '1' if the command is a composite (prefix+suffix) command, '0' otherwise.
If composite, returns the prefix part; otherwise blank.
If composite, returns the suffix part; otherwise blank.
If composite, returns the group name; otherwise blank.
Returns the category of the executing command (composite returns combined categories).
If composite, returns the prefix category.
If composite, returns the suffix category.
Returns the name of the currently loaded profile.
Returns the name of the profile that was loaded before the current one.
Returns the profile name from history (0 = current, 1 = previous, etc.). Up to 1000 entries.
Returns the name of the profile that will be loaded after the current one unloads (available only during unload).
Returns AuthorTag1 of the current profile.
Returns AuthorTag2 of the current profile.
Returns AuthorTag3 of the current profile.
Returns AuthorTag1 of the previous profile.
Returns AuthorTag2 of the previous profile.
Returns AuthorTag3 of the previous profile.
Returns AuthorTag1 of the next profile (available during unload).
Returns AuthorTag2 of the next profile.
Returns AuthorTag3 of the next profile.
Returns AuthorTag1 from profile history (0 = current, 1 = previous, etc.).
Returns AuthorTag2 from profile history.
Returns AuthorTag3 from profile history.
Returns the status of a command queue: 'Not Initialized', 'Running', 'Idle', 'Paused', or 'Stopped'.
Returns the number of commands currently in the specified queue.
Returns the name of the command currently executing in the queue, or empty if none.
Returns the total number of active command queues.
